channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Talking Sense: Coming together to solve tough problems
item.description: Mariah Levison and Rob Fersh help people find consensus on how to solve divisive issues, ranging from prison reform to what art should hang in the Minnesota Capitol. Learn how they do it in this MPR News Talking Sense conversation hosted by MPR News correspondent Catharine Richert.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, Catharine Richert, Maja Beckstrom, and Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Idaho 'abortion trafficking' law will be enforced in part, court rules
item.description: Idaho can enforce a first-of-its-kind "abortion trafficking" law, a federal court ruled, making it illegal for anyone who transports a minor across state lines to get an abortion without parental consent.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: After Trump's win, Russian disinformation aims to drive a wedge between the US and Ukraine
item.description: Russia is spreading disinformation to American audiences in an effort to erode U.S. support for Ukraine as Donald Trump prepares to return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Secret Service head vows accountability for 'abject failure' in first Trump assassination attempt
item.description: The acting director of the Secret Service is promising accountability for what he says is his agency’s “abject failure” to secure the Pennsylvania rally where a gunman opened fire on Donald Trump in July during his presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump trumps Biden as president-elect overshadows White House incumbent on world stage
item.description: Has the Oval Office been replaced by Mar-a-Lago? That's the question being asked as world leaders increasingly look to President-elect Trump rather than President Biden when it comes to diplomacy
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick wants to ban all forms of consumable THC in Texas
item.description: Senate Bill 3, which will be carried by Sen. Charles Perry, R-Lubbock, will ban all forms of THC if passed.
item.dc:creator: Christian Terry, KPRC 2

item.title: California lawmakers kick off special session to 'Trump-proof' the Golden State
item.description: California lawmakers on Monday are kicking off a special legislative session in what Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) has deemed efforts to "Trump-proof" the Golden State from potential federal interference.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth nomination sinking fast in the Senate
item.description: Senate Republicans say Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s choice to head the Defense Department, faces a very tough path to confirmation in the Senate and his bleak prospects have been communicated directly to the Trump transition team, which is now mulling other options.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis ordered to turn over communications in Trump case to conservative group
item.description: A Georgia judge ordered Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis to turn over to a conservative watchdog group any communications she may have with special counsel Jack Smith or the now-disbanded House Jan. 6 committee.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Austin endorses women in combat jobs and exhorts West Point cadets to defend the Constitution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin used a speech at West Point Wednesday to forcefully endorse having women in combat roles and emphasize the military's obligation to defend the U.S. Constitution — ideals some fear may come under fire in the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names Billy Long as IRS head
item.description: “I am pleased to announce that former Congressman Billy Long, of the Great State of Missouri, will be appointed to serve as the Commissioner of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS),” Trump wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump moves to toss Georgia criminal case as president-elect
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday asked to throw out his criminal election interference charges in Georgia following his election victory. Trump’s two federal indictments have already been tossed, and the former president is attempting to do the same in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Smiling Hunter Biden seen in first photos since receiving presidential pardon
item.description: First photos of Hunter Biden emerge after President Biden's surprise pardoning of the first son in a Sunday night announcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Trump and the Federal Reserve could clash in the coming years
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump campaigned on the promise that his policies would reduce high borrowing costs and lighten the financial burden on American households.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Rugaber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump changes White House counsel pick, tapping David Warrington
item.description: Trump in a statement said Warrington would serve as the top attorney in the White House and as assistant to the president.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump selects Jared Isaacman to lead NASA
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced he picked Jared Isaacman to lead NASA in his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says 'Africa is the future' as he pledges millions more on the last day of Angola visit
item.description: LOBITO, Angola (AP) — President Joe Biden pledged another $600 million Wednesday for an ambitious multi-country rail project in Africa as one of the final foreign policy moves of his administration, and told African leaders the resource-rich continent of more than 1.4 billion people had been “left b
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Outgoing GOP congressman seeks role as Trump's drug czar after first nominee falls through
item.description: Outgoing GOP Rep. Anthony D'Esposito, R-N.Y., is gunning to be the next leader of President-elect Donald Trump's Drug Enforcement Agency, after Trump's first nominee withdrew his name amid criticism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Hegseth ramps up Pentagon pitch with back-to-back meetings on Capitol Hill
item.description: Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump's Pentagon pick, is making rounds on Capitol Hill ahead of his nomination hearing, amid reports the former president is eyeing Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis as his replacement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP senator wants Hegseth to commit to not drinking
item.description: Sen. Kevin Cramer (R-N.D.) said that he wants President-elect Trump's pick for Defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, to stop drinking as reports mount that the former Fox News host has previously done so excessively.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s mother says The New York Times made ‘threats’ by asking her to comment on a story
item.description: A basic tenet of journalism — calling someone for comment on a story — was seen as a threat by defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth’s mother.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Louisiana's general election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Louisiana voters on Saturday will decide the fates of four proposed amendments to the state constitution, in the final statewide election of 2024.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Condo deadline looms: Owners must confront years of neglect
item.description: The condo conundrum continues as the December 31st inspection deadline is only a few weeks away. State lawmakers promise action on the issue next session, but the clock is ticking.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump appointments set off special elections to fill House seats: What to know
item.description: The move by President-elect Trump to tap several House Republicans to fill positions in his Cabinet and beyond has set off a fresh round of special elections next year to replace them. Trump chose Reps. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.) and Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) to be his national security adviser and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Canada's Trudeau says he had an 'excellent conversation' with Trump after tariffs threat
item.description: The Canadian prime minister's meeting with Donald Trump at Mar-a-Lago came after the president-elect's threat to impose tariffs on two of America's leading trade partners raised alarms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
